audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:laten + object + infinitive]] and [[grammar:moest/moesten + infinitive]]. Use [[grammar:laten]] to arrange or cause an action. Use [[grammar:moest]] or [[grammar:moesten]] for had to.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:laten + object + infinitive]]: The causer is the matrix subject, the affected participant is the object, and the lexical action is a bare infinitive.; [[grammar:moest/moesten + infinitive]]: The modal preterite locates necessity in the past while the lexical infinitive remains final.

audienceSections.normal:
#### laten + object + infinitive

Use [[grammar:laten]] to arrange or cause an action. Example: [[grammar:Ik laat Sophie de folders tellen.]]

#### moest/moesten + infinitive

Use [[grammar:moest]] or [[grammar:moesten]] for had to. Example: [[grammar:Ik moest vanmorgen vroeg naar de praktijk komen.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### laten + object + infinitive

The causer is the matrix subject, the affected participant is the object, and the lexical action is a bare infinitive. Literal example: [[grammar:Ik laat Sophie de folders tellen.]]

#### moest/moesten + infinitive

The modal preterite locates necessity in the past while the lexical infinitive remains final. Literal example: [[grammar:Ik moest vanmorgen vroeg naar de praktijk komen.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Laten]] can arrange for another person to act. [[grammar:Moest]] and [[grammar:moesten]] place obligation in the past.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Laten]] can arrange for another person to act. [[grammar:Moest]] and [[grammar:moesten]] place obligation in the past. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Ik moest vanmorgen vroeg naar de praktijk komen.]] — I had to come to the practice early this morning.
- [[grammar:Ja, ik laat Milan de meettafel klaarzetten.]] — Yes, I will have Milan set up the measurement table.
- [[grammar:Wie controleert de folders?]] — Who checks the leaflets?
- [[grammar:Ik laat Sophie de folders tellen.]] — I will have Sophie count the leaflets.
- [[grammar:Moest Yasmin de vragenlijsten meenemen?]] — Did Yasmin have to bring the questionnaires?
- [[grammar:Ja, ik moest twee dozen dragen.]] — Yes, I had to carry two boxes.
- [[grammar:Ik laat Yasmin de inschrijfbalie voorbereiden.]] — I will have Yasmin prepare the registration desk.
- [[grammar:Laat u Sophie ook de demonstratie plannen?]] — Will you also have Sophie plan the demonstration?
- [[grammar:Ja, maar eerst controleert zij de bloeddrukmeter.]] — Yes, but first she checks the blood-pressure monitor.
- [[grammar:Hoeveel folders heeft de praktijk dit jaar gebruikt?]] — How many leaflets has the practice used this year?
- [[grammar:Ongeveer vijfduizend.]] — About five thousand.
- [[grammar:Moest u vroeger alles op papier schrijven?]] — Did you use to have to write everything on paper?
- [[grammar:Ja, maar nu laat ik het systeem de formulieren opslaan.]] — Yes, but now I have the system save the forms.
- [[grammar:Wanneer begint de gezondheidsdag?]] — When does the health information day begin?
- [[grammar:Over twee weken.]] — In two weeks.
- [[grammar:Dan laat ik de datum in de agenda zetten.]] — Then I will have the date entered in the planner.
- [[grammar:Goed, ik moet de inschrijfbalie voorbereiden.]] — Good, I have to prepare the registration desk.
- [[grammar:Mooi, dan is het plan voor vandaag klaar.]] — Great, then today’s plan is finished.
